name: Matarraña Valleys Trail (PR-Z 162)
name_original: De Mealla a Tossal Gort — circular por el Río Matarraña
description: Enjoy an easy 3.7-mile circular hike from Mealla to Tossal Gort, following the scenic Río Matarraña with river views and varied landscapes.
region_intro: Hiking around Maella offers trails through a landscape characterized by hills, river valleys, and historical sites. The region features paths that traverse varied terrain, including areas with ancient Iberian settlements and natural viewpoints. These routes provide opportunities to explore the local geology and cultural heritage. The trails often follow natural contours, offering a mix of gentle gradients and moderate ascents. Best hiking trails around Maella The most popular hiking route is Poblado ibérico de San Antonio y Tossal Redó — circular desde Calaceite, an 8.2 miles (13.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 34 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores an Iberian settlement and a local hill. Another top favourite among local hikers is San Antonio Iberian Settlement and San Cristóbal Hill Trail, an easy 2.0 miles (3.3 km) path. This trail leads through an Iberian settlement and ascends San Cristóbal Hill, offering views of the surrounding area. Local hikers also love the Matarraña Valleys Trail (PR-Z 162), a 3.7 miles (6.0 km) trail leading through the Matarraña Valleys, often completed in about 1 hour 35 minutes. Hiking around Maella is defined by Iberian settlements, river valleys, and local hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate hikes. The routes in Maella are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from 4 reviews. More than 36 hikers have used komoot to explore Maella's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking around Maella?
A: Hiking around Maella features a diverse landscape of hills, river valleys, and historical sites. Trails often traverse areas with ancient Iberian settlements and offer natural viewpoints. The routes follow natural contours, providing a mix of gentle gradients and moderate ascents, reflecting the local geology and cultural heritage.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Maella?
A: Yes, Maella offers several easy trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1323747491/san-antonio-iberian-settlement-and-san-cristobal-hill-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Antonio Iberian Settlement and San Cristóbal Hill Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.3 km path that leads through an Iberian settlement and ascends San Cristóbal Hill. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169689584/matarrana-valleys-trail-pr-z-162" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matarraña Valleys Trail (PR-Z 162)&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.0 km long.

Q: What are some notable historical or cultural sites to see on the trails?
A: Maella's trails are rich in historical significance. You can explore ancient Iberian settlements, such as on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1168833093/poblado-iberico-de-san-antonio-y-tossal-redo-circular-desde-calaceite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poblado ibérico de San Antonio y Tossal Redó — circular desde Calaceite&lt;/a&gt; route. Another trail, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1168646965/loma-de-los-brunos-necropolis-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loma de los Brunos Necropolis Trail&lt;/a&gt;, takes you past an ancient necropolis. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1168679483/puente-de-masatrigos-by-the-monument-to-the-international-brigades-round-trip-via-the-camino-jacobeo-del-ebro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Masatrigos&lt;/a&gt; route passes by a monument to the International Brigades.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Maella?
A: Yes, there are circular routes available. A prominent example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1168833093/poblado-iberico-de-san-antonio-y-tossal-redo-circular-desde-calaceite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poblado ibérico de San Antonio y Tossal Redó — circular desde Calaceite&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 13.1 km loop exploring an Iberian settlement and a local hill.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Maella?
A: The hiking routes in Maella are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from 4 reviews. Hikers often appreciate the varied terrain, the historical sites, and the opportunities to explore the local landscape.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Maella?
A: There are over 10 hiking trails available around Maella, offering a range of options from easy walks to more moderate hikes.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or natural features to look out for?
A: Many trails offer natural viewpoints over the surrounding area, particularly from local hills like San Cristóbal. The region's landscape is characterized by river valleys, such as those explored on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169689584/matarrana-valleys-trail-pr-z-162" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matarraña Valleys Trail (PR-Z 162)&lt;/a&gt;, providing scenic natural features.

Q: What are some interesting places to explore near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the trails, you can find several interesting attractions. These include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2752464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Equinox Tunnel&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3560752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Railway Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;. There are also historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3588537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abandoned Valljunquera Railway Station&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3549343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abandoned Valldeltormo Station&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of hikes in Maella?
A: The hikes in Maella range from easy to moderate difficulty. There are 4 easy trails and 6 moderate trails, making the area accessible for various fitness levels. There are no difficult trails listed in the immediate vicinity.

Q: What is the longest hiking trail available in Maella?
A: The longest hiking trail listed is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1168833093/poblado-iberico-de-san-antonio-y-tossal-redo-circular-desde-calaceite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poblado ibérico de San Antonio y Tossal Redó — circular desde Calaceite&lt;/a&gt;, which covers a distance of 13.1 km and typically takes about 3 hours and 34 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there any trails that follow old railway lines or tunnels?
A: Yes, some trails and nearby attractions suggest a connection to old railway infrastructure. You can find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3560752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Railway Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3588537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abandoned Valljunquera Railway Station&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3549343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abandoned Valldeltormo Station&lt;/a&gt;, indicating paths that might utilize former railway routes.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the From Mealla to Tossal Gort circular hike?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting and ending in Mealla. You can typically find parking within the village to begin your hike.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route is generally considered an easy walk, making it suitable for beginners and families. The varied terrain and scenic river views offer an enjoyable experience for all.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on this hike?
faqs.answer: You'll experience varied terrain, including river meadows, sections with thick gravel and river stones, crop landscapes, Mediterranean scrub, and olive groves. Some stretches also pass through pine forests. The path closely follows the Matarraña river.

faqs.question: What are some notable sights or features along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the way, you can enjoy the distinct character of the Matarraña river's middle course. You'll also cross the provincial border between Teruel and Zaragoza. Keep an eye out for the View of Maella from Tossal Gort, and if riverside vegetation permits, you might catch glimpses of Maella's castle and clock tower. The area is also rich in history, with paths leading to Iberian archaeological sites.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route partially overlaps with other official trails. Approximately 36% of the route follows the PR-Z 162. Vales del Matarraña, and about 12% coincides with the Pas de les Xerisses trail.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular hike?
faqs.answer: The hike is approximately 6 kilometers long and typically takes around 1 hour and 35 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is generally accessible, specific rules regarding dogs (leash requirements, waste disposal) may vary. It's always best to check local regulations or signage upon arrival, especially since the area is known for livestock like the "Maellana" sheep.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during various seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, so an early start is recommended, while winter might offer a different, quieter charm.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to hike this specific trail. It's a publicly accessible route.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants nearby in Mealla?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the trail starts and ends in Mealla, you can find local amenities such as cafes and restaurants in the village to refresh yourself before or after your hike.

faqs.question: Are there river crossings, and is water available on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route involves crossing the Matarraña river multiple times, sometimes via fords. It's advisable to carry sufficient water with you, as there are no designated water sources directly on the trail.

waypoints.name: View of Maella from Tossal Gort
